items.title: Two U.S. service members killed in Jordan, one missing; Iran threatens 'unforgettable lessons' after calling off interim peace agreement
items.description: When asked about Iran no longer abiding by the interim peace agreement, U.S. President Donald Trump reportedly said, "I couldn't care less."

items.title: Taylor Farms recalls iceberg lettuce in 27 states due to cyclosporiasis outbreak
items.description: Taylor Farms is recalling iceberg lettuce in 27 states because of the potential that the greens could be contaminated with cyclosporiasis.

items.title: 27-year-old locksmith spent $7,000 on tools, works around the clock in NYC: 'I'd never held a screwdriver in my life' before starting
items.description: Yona Sahar, founder of Locksmith Girl of NYC, spent two years learning the trade. Now, she runs her own business while working around the clock.

items.title: Korean beauty products are becoming mainstream in the U.S. Why there may be even more growth ahead
items.description: Morgan Stanley forecasts that K-beauty sales in the U.S. can reach approximately $4 billion in 2026.

items.title: 'WarshGPT': How Wall Street is adapting to the Fed's new era of communication
items.description: Investment firms are readying for an era with less public commentary from the U.S. central bank. Some are relying on AI for an edge.

items.title: The AI rotation stole the spotlight from a strong start to earnings season
items.description: The AI trade dominated the market once again this week, upstaging an impressive start to the second-quarter earnings season.

items.title: Fanatics CEO Michael Rubin on two sports businesses his multibillion-dollar conglomerate won't enter
items.description: Fanatics has become one of the most influential companies in sports, building out a massive merchandise, collectibles and sports gambling platform.

items.title: Two unexpected revelations in Warren Buffett's CNBC interview
items.description: Warren Buffett tells CNBC that Bill Gates' ties to Jeffrey Epstein didn't drive his termination of donations.

items.title: Why Dana White thinks much more of UFC's future will be Power Slap and boxing
items.description: UFC president and CEO Dana White predicted that Power Slap may become as big as his flagship fighting sport at the Game Plan conference in New York.

items.title: Scam victims can owe taxes on stolen money. A bill in Congress could offer relief
items.description: A bill in the House would restore a pre-2018 rule that generally allowed a tax deduction for theft losses and would add other relief for fraud victims.

items.title: I left the U.S. for Trinidad and Tobago: My family of 4 spends about $3,000 a month—and we've never been happier
items.description: Chantel Henry lives with her husband and two kids in Trinidad. She says living there has made her American Dream more affordable.

items.title: World Cup audience stayed bipartisan despite Trump's prominent role, CNBC survey finds
items.description: Nearly half of registered voters watched the 2026 World Cup, with income and education shaping viewership more than party affiliation, the survey found.

items.title: How are prediction markets taxed? The IRS hasn’t provided guidance yet
items.description: Experts say the lack of federal guidance makes it's unclear on how prediction markets winnings should be reported and levied.

items.title: Fat cat? Weight-loss drugs may be coming for your overweight pet
items.description: Startups are testing GLP-1 treatments for overweight cats while pet-food giants are leaning into longevity products as owners spend more on animal health.

items.title: Chinese AI has leveled up, and brought renewed focus on the open weight model shift
items.description: It's the latest AI model from China to close the performance gap with leading U.S. AI labs.

items.title: Leather jacket worn by Nvidia CEO goes for just under $1 million at Sotheby's auction
items.description: The high price for the garment is a sign that collectors are looking to bid on artifacts and collectibles from the artificial intelligence boom.

items.title: The White House is dictating access to frontier AI models, shifting power from tech giants, sources say
items.description: The Trump administration is taking steps to control who gets access to the latest frontier models, people familiar with the matter told CNBC.

items.title: Trump rips Canada as wildfire smoke spreads, says U.S. will add pollution cost to tariffs
items.description: The smoke coming from active wildfires in Canada has led to unhealthy air quality in swaths of the U.S., which could affect the World Cup final in New Jersey.

items.title: Taco Bell says it has removed lettuce linked to cyclosporiasis outbreak from its restaurants
items.description: Taco Bell, which the CDC linked to the cyclosporiasis outbreak, will likely recover soon from the health safety scare, according to analysts.

items.title: SpaceX stock has cratered nearly 23% since the company joined the Nasdaq-100
items.description: SpaceX's stock plunged further on Friday, a day after it aborted a test flight for its Starship rocket at the last second.
